eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors James Harden at 37.5% implied probability for Eastern Conference Finals MVP, closely trailed by Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram at 35% and Cavaliers teammate Donovan Mitchell at 29%, reflecting the tight 3-2 series lead Cleveland holds over Toronto in their first-round matchup after a 125-120 Game 5 comeback victory on April 29 driven by late surges from Dennis Schröder and Evan Mobley alongside Harden's veteran playmaking. Ingram's scoring outbursts, including 23 points in Game 4's upset win that knotted the series at 2-2, keep Raptors' upset potential alive heading into Game 6. With the Knicks advancing decisively 4-2 over Atlanta via a record 51-point Game 6 rout and Boston-Philadelphia tied 3-3 pending Game 7, multiple paths to conference semifinals amplify uncertainty, distributing probabilities across stars from surviving contenders amid no major injury concerns.

markets.question: Gaza flotilla enters Israeli waters by May 31?
markets.description:
A flotilla organized by the Global Sumud Flotilla, among others, set sail from Spain on April 15, 2026 to deliver aid to Gaza (see: https://apnews.com/article/global-sumud-flotilla-gaza-aid-spain-israel-94b09412fdcb1a0fd6a6e0c981479539). 

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any vessel which is part of this flotilla enters Israel’s territorial sea by May 31, 2026 (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market, Israel’s territorial sea includes the territorial waters off the coast of Gaza that are controlled by Israel.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Gaza flotilla enters Israeli waters by May 31?
events.description:
A flotilla organized by the Global Sumud Flotilla, among others, set sail from Spain on April 15, 2026 to deliver aid to Gaza (see: https://apnews.com/article/global-sumud-flotilla-gaza-aid-spain-israel-94b09412fdcb1a0fd6a6e0c981479539). 

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any vessel which is part of this flotilla enters Israel’s territorial sea by May 31, 2026 (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market, Israel’s territorial sea includes the territorial waters off the coast of Gaza that are controlled by Israel.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli naval forces intercepted multiple Gaza-bound aid flotillas, including the Global Sumud Flotilla, Thousand Madleens to Gaza, and Freedom Flotilla Coalition vessels, in international waters near Crete on April 29-30, detaining over 175 activists and diverting ships away from the region. This preemptive action, hundreds of miles from Gaza, echoes Israel's longstanding enforcement of its maritime blockade amid the ongoing Gaza conflict, preventing any approach to Israeli territorial waters. With vessels seized and crews facing deportation or questioning, traders price a 67% implied probability on "No," reflecting consensus that no flotilla will breach the blockade by the May 31 deadline absent major diplomatic shifts or new initiatives.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 82.5% implied probability to no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting consensus that the current 3.50%-3.75% federal funds target range—held steady at the April 29 FOMC meeting—remains sufficiently restrictive amid balanced risks. March CPI inflation accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year, the highest since May 2024, yet unemployment held steady at 4.3% with modest 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains, tempering hike pressures. The March dot plot median projects a year-end 2026 rate of 3.4%, implying potential easing rather than tightening, while futures markets price a flat path around 3.6%. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI on May 12 and the June FOMC, where persistent supply shocks could influence sentiment.

Watch 12Z model runs and NWS updates Friday afternoon for shifts.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Starlink's explosive subscriber growth to over 10 million and record launch cadence—surpassing 100 Falcon missions last year—alongside Starship's orbital test successes, have fueled this momentum, outpacing rivals like Blue Origin and United Launch Alliance. Recent xAI merger integration hints at AI-enhanced mission planning, while accelerated employee share vesting signals imminent public debut; watch for the late-May prospectus release as the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 74% implied probability against the U.S. enacting an AI safety bill before 2027, driven by federal legislation remaining stalled in the 119th Congress despite numerous introductions like the AI Guardrails Act and bipartisan reauthorizations for the NIST AI Safety Institute. The Trump administration's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework urged light-touch regulation and state preemption, but elicited limited response amid partisan debates over regulatory scope. Recent progress, such as the GUARD Act unanimously passing Senate Judiciary Committee on April 30 to curb harmful AI chatbots for minors, highlights incremental movement overshadowed by states' rapid enactments in Connecticut, Minnesota, and Tennessee. With eight months left before resolution, historical gridlock on comprehensive tech bills sustains skepticism for floor passage, committee advancement, or presidential signature.

markets.question: Will monthly inflation increase by ≥1.1% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in April 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro as Venezuela's head of state by December 31, 2026, reflecting the market's resolution via the UN-recognized government's official designation, where his January capture by U.S. forces has not yet shifted international listings despite four months of Delcy Rodríguez's acting presidency. Recent U.S. sanction relief on Rodríguez in early April, resumed IMF dealings, and first commercial flights from Miami signal stabilizing regime continuity under her leadership, elevating her to second place amid purges of Maduro allies. Opposition leader María Corina Machado's April 20 announcement of a planned return and demands for swift elections introduces uncertainty, though her odds trail amid absent election timelines and constitutional interim limits exceeded without National Assembly action.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 35°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 33°C (29%) and 32°C (25.5%) for Taipei's highest temperature on May 3, driven by Central Weather Administration (CWA) forecasts and global models like ECMWF and GFS projecting highs in the low-to-mid 30s°C under a developing upper-level ridge promoting subsidence and clear skies. Recent showers through May 1-2 have capped daytime maxima around 27-30°C, but anticipated reduction in cloud cover and strong afternoon solar heating—amplified by Taipei's urban heat island—favor above-normal peaks near climatological May averages of 29°C. markets.question: Will Meta have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Theo FDV above $1B one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Theo's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Theo (https://x.com/Theo_Network) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Travis Kelce retire before next season?
markets.description:

An official announcement from Kelce that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred, as long as the announced retirement is stated to take effect before the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season. Announcements that Kelce will retire after the start of the 2026-2027 NFL regular season will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Travis Kelce, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Eurozone annual GDP growth in 2026 be at least 7.0%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the estimation of Euro Area (Eurozone) annual GDP growth for the full year of 2026 (% change), based on seasonally and calendar adjusted quarterly data, as reported in the "GDP and employment flash estimates for the fourth quarter of 2026" flash release for Q4 of 2026, scheduled to be released in January 2027.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/euro-indicators

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the Euro Area GDP growth rate for the full year of 2026 is included in this release, this market will resolve according to the Euro Area GDP growth rate for Q4 2026, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year. If no data is released for either the full year or fourth quarter of 2026 by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced flash GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release of the specified report will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Eurozone 2026 annual GDP growth in a tight race between 0-1.0% (35.7% implied probability) and 1.0-2.0% (34.5%), reflecting uncertainty over a softening economic trajectory amid recent headwinds. Yesterday's Eurostat flash estimate showed Q1 2026 GDP expanding just 0.1% quarter-on-quarter—below the 0.2% consensus and prior 0.2% pace—driven by surging energy costs from Middle East tensions and weakening external demand. This follows IMF's April downward revision to 1.1% for 2026 (from 1.4%) and ECB staff's March cut by 0.3 percentage points, citing trade disruptions. Key swing factors include ECB policy easing at upcoming June FOMC-equivalent meetings, Q2 indicators, and inflation trajectory; sub-1% odds could rise if energy shocks persist, while fiscal support might tilt toward 1-2%.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: WI-06 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman’s strong track record in the R+8 Wisconsin 6th Congressional District, where he won 61% in 2024 amid Donald Trump’s 58% district margin, anchors trader consensus at 81% for a Republican victory. The crowded Democratic primary field of eight candidates—including Amanda Bell and Bradley Smith—threatens to produce a fragmented, underfunded nominee, with the top Democrat holding just $53,000 cash on hand versus Grothman’s $700,000. Independents Michael Thurow and Elizabeth Fitzgibbon pose minimal threats in this safe Republican seat per Cook Political Report and Sabato’s Crystal Ball ratings. Recent Grothman legislative wins, like his Federal Fraud Prevention bill advancing April 30, reinforce incumbency advantages ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas wins 3 or more games than Toronto KOI in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Toronto KOI". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TEX (-2.5) vs Toronto KOI (+2.5)

events.title: Call of Duty: Toronto KOI vs OpTic Texas (BO5) - Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers
events.description:
This market refers to the Call of Duty match between Toronto KOI and OpTic Texas in the Call of Duty League Stage 3 Major Qualifiers Qualifiers, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Toronto KOI" if Toronto KOI win the match against OpTic Texas.

This market will resolve to "OpTic Texas" if OpTic Texas win the match against Toronto KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/callofduty/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpTic Texas, the CDL standings leader with 310 points, holds trader consensus edge over Toronto KOI (7th, 160 points) in their high-stakes BO5 Stage 3 Major Qualifiers matchup on May 2, driven by a dominant 3-1 elimination win over KOI in the April Minor I tournament and superior scrim results. Both teams remain unbeaten in qualifiers—Toronto 2-0 series (6-2 maps), OpTic 1-0 (3-1 maps)—positioning the winner for Major bracket advancement amid the Black Ops 6 meta favoring OpTic's elite hardpoint efficiency (top win rate). Toronto's mid-season addition of Kips bolsters their roster rebound, but slayer inconsistencies, especially around Insight, persist as a vulnerability, while no roster changes or patches alter the landscape.

markets.question: Will Steve Hilton finish first in the 2026 California Governor primary election?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the most valid votes in this primary election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent debate performances and Tom Steyer's unprecedented $132 million self-funded advertising blitz have edged him to trader consensus front-runner status at 36.5% implied probability for first place in California's June 2 nonpartisan top-two primary, narrowly ahead of Xavier Becerra's 31.5% surge fueled by his attorney general and cabinet experience amid Eric Swalwell's mid-April exit and Betty Yee's late withdrawal. Steve Hilton trails closely at 29.5%, buoyed by conservative backlash to Democratic dominance on housing costs, crime, and the economy—top voter concerns in Emerson and CBS polls showing fragmented support across a crowded field of over 10 candidates. The race remains fluid with low name recognition; separation could come from Newsom endorsements, final polls, or independent turnout shifts before early voting ramps up.

markets.question: Will John Thune be the next Senate Majority Leader?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the individual who is announced as the next Senate Majority Leader after the November 3, 2026, U.S. General Election.

This market will remain open until January 3, 2027, at which point it will resolve based on the first announcement of the next Senate Majority Leader from whichever party holds the majority in the U.S. Senate.

If no Majority is established and a leader is not announced by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Republicans hold the Senate majority under Leader John Thune following their 2024 gains, but traders price a 67% implied probability of Democrats regaining control after the November 2026 midterms, elevating Chuck Schumer to 43.5% as the top outcome despite intra-party murmurs of challengers like Brian Schatz at 12%. This sentiment stems from the favorable map—GOP defending 22 seats to Democrats' 13—and a mid-April shift in betting odds flipping Democratic Senate odds to 52% amid tightening polls in battleground races. Schumer's April assertions of flipping the chamber bolster his position, while Thune's 29% reflects GOP retention paths narrowed by historical midterm headwinds for the president's party and recent DHS funding negotiations highlighting leadership dynamics. Primaries in competitive states loom as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects uncertainty in Elon Musk's X posting pace for May 2026, with top bins—1160-1199 (10%) and 780-799 (9%)—highlighting a split between expecting an April-like average of ~35 daily posts (yielding ~1085 total) or the late-April slowdown to ~27 per day amid his OpenAI trial testimony. April resolved around 1050-1070 posts per prior markets, but the April 24-May 1 stretch tallied just 188 (~27/day), tempering optimism despite spikes like 38 on April 27. Swing factors include imminent catalysts: Tesla Cybercab unveils, xAI Grok updates, SpaceX milestones, and viral political commentary, any of which could ignite higher-volume weeks as traders wager on his signature event-driven engagement surges.

markets.question: Will Roberto Vannacci be the next Prime Minister of Italy?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Italy by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Italy. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Italy; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Giorgia Meloni leads trader consensus at 47% implied probability to serve as Italy's next prime minister, anchored by Brothers of Italy's polling dominance around 29% and her coalition's firm parliamentary majority despite the March 2026 judicial reform referendum defeat that spurred opposition momentum without triggering a no-confidence vote. Roberto Vannacci's 15% odds arise from his February defection from Lega to form a far-right splinter, challenging Meloni from the right and drawing voter interest. Elly Schlein (12.5%) and Giuseppe Conte (10%) represent centre-left continuity, while Genoa Mayor Silvia Salis (9.9%) surges on recent visibility as a potential opposition unifier. Absent snap election signals, the next general election looms no earlier than December 2027 amid energy crisis strains.

markets.question: Spread: Getafe CF (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:15 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Getafe CF" if Getafe CF win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Rayo Vallecano de Madrid".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Getafe CF vs. Rayo Vallecano de Madrid - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Getafe, sitting 8th in La Liga with 41 points after 31 matches, hosts 13th-placed Rayo Vallecano (35 points) in a pivotal Madrid derby at the Coliseum Alfonso Pérez on May 3, seeking to bolster European qualification hopes amid a tight mid-table race. Getafe's attack is hampered by injuries to key forwards Borja Mayoral and Juanmi, plus suspended defender Zaid Romero, while Rayo miss center-back Luiz Felipe and winger Fran Pérez. Rayo remain unbeaten in the last 10 head-to-heads (3 wins, 7 draws), including January's 1-1 stalemate, underscoring their derby resilience despite Rayo's leakier defense (38 goals conceded). Both sides average modest scoring in recent form, with favorable 25°C weather unlikely to intervene.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Tampa Bay Rays (14-11, second in AL East) host the struggling San Francisco Giants (11-14, fourth in NL West) in an interleague series opener at dome-covered Tropicana Field on May 1, with trader consensus likely favoring the hotter Rays after their 7-3 mark over the last 10 games compared to San Francisco's middling 5-5 stretch. Key pitching matchup features Giants lefty Robbie Ray (2-3, 2.86 ERA) against Rays southpaw Shane McClanahan (1-2, 5.00 ERA), but San Francisco's injury report looms large with recent ailments sidelining catcher Daniel Susac (elbow), outfielder Jung Hoo Lee (leg), and others like Harrison Bader (hamstring), thinning their lineup and outfield depth. Rays hold a slight all-time edge (13-11 head-to-head) and home-field advantage, though both bullpens could prove decisive in a tight contest amid early-season volatility.

markets.question: Will the April 2026 unemployment rate be 4.4%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in the Employment Situation Report for April 2026.

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for the month in question.

The relevant data release is scheduled for May 8, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. This market will resolve as soon as the relevant data is issued. Any revisions to the data after the first release will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a tight contest for the April unemployment rate, with 4.3% leading at 31% implied probability, closely trailed by 4.2% (25.5%) and 4.4% (22.5%), reflecting a stable labor market after March's surprise decline to 4.3%—below the expected 4.4%—amid +178,000 nonfarm payrolls. Recent initial jobless claims plunged to a near-record low of 189,000 for the week ending April 25, signaling minimal layoffs, while ADP private payrolls averaged modest gains around 40,000 weekly in early April and Chicago Fed nowcasts eased to 4.23%. Differentiating factors include household survey volatility versus establishment data strength; the BLS report due today could swing outcomes based on participation rate shifts or revisions.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Democrats at 60.5% to win the 2028 US presidential election, reflecting recent declines in President Trump's approval rating to a second-term low of 34% amid the onset of the Iran war, surging oil-driven inflation with gas at $4.12 per gallon, and broader global instability. These pressures have fueled anti-incumbent sentiment against Republicans after eight years in the White House, compounded by term limits barring Trump's re-run in the open-seat race. Historical patterns show the party holding the presidency often struggles in such cycles, with traders also eyeing Democratic momentum in 2026 midterm markets for House and Senate control as a preview of battleground turnout ahead of November 2028.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the B.C. Conservative Party leadership election reflects a tight race among the final five candidates, with Kerry-Lynne Findlay's federal ministerial experience under Stephen Harper giving her a narrow edge at 36.3% over Caroline Elliott's 31%, mirroring recent Research Co. polls showing no clear frontrunner and high undecideds among 2024 Conservative voters. The April 24 debate at the Canada Strong and Free Network conference intensified clashes over social conservatism, Indigenous policy like DRIPA repeal, and unity pacts such as Yuri Fulmer's OneBC deal, polarizing Elliott and Fulmer while positioning Findlay as even-handed. Peter Milobar's incumbent MLA status holds third at 16%, buoyed by electability polls. Ongoing member identity verification through May 20 ahead of May 23-29 online ranked-choice voting could shift dynamics via late endorsements or turnout, emphasizing second preferences in this preferential ballot system.

eventMetadata.context_description: Annie Andrews holds a commanding 91% implied probability as the South Carolina Democratic U.S. Senate primary frontrunner, driven by her overwhelming fundraising dominance—$6.5 million in total receipts through March 31 per FEC data, dwarfing rivals like Kyle Freeman's $53,000 and Catherine Fleming Bruce's negligible totals—and statewide name recognition from her 2022 congressional campaign. markets.question: Will there be exactly 3 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from April 27 - May 3?

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be $1.5T or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Evo Morales arrested by May 31
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Evo Morales, former President of Bolivia, is arrested or detained by law enforcement between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Evo Morales arrested by May 31
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Evo Morales, former President of Bolivia, is arrested or detained by law enforcement between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Despite longstanding arrest warrants for former Bolivian President Evo Morales on human trafficking and statutory rape charges stemming from 2024 investigations, traders price an 81% chance he evades detention by May 31, reflecting the absence of enforcement actions in recent weeks. Morales, a MAS party leader, remains at large in his Chapare stronghold, shielded by loyal cocalero supporters amid risks of violent clashes. The right-wing government under President Rodrigo Paz, facing economic pressures post-2025 elections, has prioritized other issues, with officials signaling no imminent pursuit as of late April. His February reappearance after a seven-week absence quelled flight rumors but prompted no raids, underscoring political caution ahead of potential unrest.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 17°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ensemble forecasts from ECMWF and GFS models project Moscow's highest temperature on May 2 clustering around 14°C under persistent overcast skies and lingering Arctic air from the record-breaking April 27-28 snowstorm, which shattered late-spring snowfall records and suppressed early May warming. Trader consensus mirrors this tight model spread, with 33.5% implied probability on 14°C edging out 15°C (25.2%) and 13°C (22.5%) due to sensitivity to cloud cover thickness, weak upper-level ridging, and potential afternoon clearing. Historical early-May highs average 15°C, but this year's anomalous cold pool introduces uncertainty; new 12z model runs and Roshydromet updates today could shift odds further ahead of evening resolution via official Vnukovo Airport observations.

markets.question: Will Tarik Skubal strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal lead the Premier League table on 73 points after 34 games, three clear of Manchester City—who hold a game in hand at 70 points—following their recent Haaland-inspired win at Burnley, while Manchester United sit third on 61 points ahead of Liverpool and Aston Villa, both on 58. United's victory over Brentford and Liverpool's climb past Palace have intensified the battle for Champions League spots with four matches remaining. Key upcoming fixtures include Liverpool vs. Manchester United, Fulham vs. Arsenal, Everton vs. City, and Tottenham vs. Villa, where slips could allow challengers to close gaps amid injury concerns like Salah's fitness and Cunha's availability. Traders eye form, goal difference edges (+38 Arsenal, +37 City), and head-to-head results in this tightly contested race.

markets.question: Joel Embiid: Points O/U 26.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 2 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Joel Embiid scores more than 26.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Joel Embiid scores 26.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: 76ers vs. Celtics
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 2 at 12:00AM ET:
If the 76ers win, the market will resolve to "76ers".
If the Celtics win, the market will resolve to "Celtics".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Boston Celtics welcome the Philadelphia 76ers to TD Garden for a winner-take-all Game 7 in their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series, holding a 3-2 lead after Philadelphia's dominant 106-93 Game 6 win on April 30. Jayson Tatum exited that loss with left calf discomfort but is expected available per Shams Charania, preserving Boston's core alongside home-court advantage and superior regular-season form against the seventh seed. Joel Embiid, back strong post-appendectomy with 19 points, 10 rebounds, and eight assists in Game 6, anchors the 76ers' surge, having limited the Celtics below 100 points in consecutive victories amid Tyrese Maxey's 30-point outburst. Momentum favors Philadelphia's resilience, but Boston's playoff pedigree and crowd support shape trader consensus.

markets.description:

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

eventMetadata.context_description: PAGASA's latest extended forecast, issued April 30, projects a 36°C high for Metro Manila on May 3 amid mostly clear skies and light easterly winds, fueling the market's 38.5% implied probability for that outcome over 35°C at 31%. This aligns with May's climatological peak as the Philippines' hottest month, where historical highs average 34°C but routinely exceed 36°C due to urban heat island effects and subsidence aloft. Trader sentiment reflects uncertainty from a 10% chance of isolated thunderstorms or enhanced sea breezes potentially capping temperatures at 35°C, versus persistent dry conditions from the recent El Niño alert (raised April 22) boosting odds for 37°C. Watch PAGASA's daily update on May 2 for refined model consensus.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the TX-15 House seat?

This South Texas battleground remains tightly contested due to De La Cruz's proven incumbency advantage, 57% victory in 2024, superior fundraising ($2.3 million cash on hand vs. Pulido's $400,000 as of March), and focus on border security resonating in the Rio Grande Valley. New polls, endorsements, turnout among Hispanic voters, or midterm national trends could tip the balance before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's second-term push for greater U.S. influence in Greenland, renewed in January 2026 via tariff threats against opposing NATO allies and a Davos "framework" for future security cooperation, has yielded no sovereignty transfer despite heightened rhetoric on Arctic national security amid Russia and China competition. Denmark and Greenland officials, backed by massive Nuuk protests, reiterated non-negotiable sovereignty, while legal analyses highlight obstacles under international law and the 1951 Defense of Greenland Agreement limiting U.S. rights to bases like Thule. April Pentagon talks for expanded military access to three additional areas signal de-escalation toward cooperation, not acquisition, driving trader consensus to an 85.5% "No" probability for the year.

markets.question: Newcastle United FC vs. Brighton & Hove Albion FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Newcastle United FC and Brighton & Hove Albion FC, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Newcastle United FC and Brighton & Hove Albion FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Newcastle United host Brighton & Hove Albion at St. James' Park in Premier League Matchweek 35, with both sides vying in the mid-table scrum—Newcastle around 14th after 33 games amid a tough run including recent losses to Crystal Palace and Bournemouth, while Brighton sit higher near 9th following a 2-2 draw at Tottenham. Newcastle's injury woes persist, with captain Bruno Guimarães pushing for a return from hamstring trouble and illness after two months out, though Fabian Schär's foot infection sidelines him into early May; Brighton's list is lighter despite Diego Gómez's recent knock. Home advantage and potential returns could boost the Magpies, but Brighton's stronger recent form and head-to-head edge, including a 2-1 win last October, shape trader consensus on a competitive clash.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In a tightly contested Eastern Conference first-round playoff series where the eighth-seeded Orlando Magic hold a 3-2 lead over the top-seeded Detroit Pistons, Game 6 at Kia Center carries elimination stakes for Detroit. Franz Wagner's confirmed absence due to a right calf strain—his second straight missed game—forces lineup adjustments around Paolo Banchero, while Jonathan Isaac remains sidelined with a knee sprain. Pistons gained momentum from Cade Cunningham's franchise playoff-record 45 points in their 116-109 Game 5 road win, but Tobias Harris is questionable with a left ankle sprain, and Ausar Thompson nursed an ankle tweak. Home-court advantage and Orlando's defensive edge in the series temper Detroit's upset potential amid these injury uncertainties.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will not shake hands by June 30, driven by entrenched disagreements over territorial withdrawals, annexed regions, and full Russian troop pullouts amid ongoing military stalemate. Recent developments, including Putin's conditional openness to a meeting only for finalizing agreements (April 22) and proposals for a short-term May 9 Victory Day ceasefire discussed with U.S. President Trump (late April), have yielded no progress toward direct talks, with Zelenskyy seeking details but rejecting key Russian demands. Absent a major diplomatic breakthrough—such as successful U.S.-mediated negotiations or de-escalation signals—the timeline leaves insufficient room for personal diplomacy.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Beijing be 29°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Beijing Capital International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Beijing Capital International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/beijing/ZBAA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has locked in at virtually 100% for a highest temperature of 26°C in Beijing on May 1, driven by official observations from Beijing Capital International Airport—the market's key resolution source—recording a peak of 79°F (equivalent to 26°C) between noon and 3 p.m. local time, followed by steady cooling under partly cloudy skies with light winds. China Meteorological Administration forecasts aligned precisely with this outcome, projecting a 26°C high amid typical early-May climatological norms of 25–28°C daytime maxima. While inherent measurement uncertainties exist, a late-day surge to 27°C+ remains implausible given declining solar insolation and evening temperatures already below 23°C; final CMA daily summaries expected soon will confirm, but trader capital reflects overwhelming evidence-based certainty.

markets.question: Will Sweetgreen (SG) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Sweetgreen is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Sweetgreen's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.23 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sweetgreen reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.23 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Sweetgreen releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

Recent stock gains of ~36% over the past month reflect shifting sentiment on operational tweaks, with the earnings call as the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 disclosure of 287 nominees for the 2026 Peace Prize has intensified trader sentiment on Polymarket, where Yulia Navalnaya holds a slim 8.5% implied probability lead through her enduring anti-Putin activism and poignant widow-of-martyr narrative, resonating with the committee's history of honoring dissidents amid Russia's aggression. Volodymyr Zelenskyy trails closely at 7.8%, bolstered by his confirmed nomination alongside Ukraine's people for defending European democracies during the protracted war. Donald Trump sits at 6.5% on high-volume bets fueled by head-of-state endorsements from Pakistan, Cambodia, and Israel for diplomatic de-escalations like the 2025 India-Pakistan crisis. This fragmented field reflects geopolitical uncertainty, with UNRWA's Gaza aid efforts and Qatar's Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani's mediation drawing support; watch for summer breakthroughs to shift the closely contested dynamics before the October 9 announcement.

Bundesliga matches, including a 1-0 victory over Hertha BSC—positions them as trader consensus favorites at 43.5% implied probability for the home win against relegation-threatened Eintracht Braunschweig, who sit 14th and face 50-50 survival odds with three games left. markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day by December 31, 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2027".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Mohamed Salah play in Scottish Premiership next?

If Mohamed Salah signs a contract that includes a delayed transfer or loan-back clause requiring him to remain at Liverpool for a specified period before joining another club, this market will resolve to the club he is officially contracted to join following that period.

If Mohamed Salah joins a club in a league that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

Transfers or assignments between a club’s senior team and reserve or affiliate teams will not be considered for this market.

An official transfer announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from Liverpool, Mohamed Salah and/or the acquiring club. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

markets.question: Will OpenAI announce a head-mounted display in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to "Yes" if the listed type of consumer hardware product is publicly announced by OpenAI by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announcement of a qualifying consumer hardware product within the market timeframe will suffice, regardless of whether or when the product is released.

If a device fulfills multiple device categories, then all relevant categories will resolve to "Yes".

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

events.title: What kind of product will OpenAI announce in 2026?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors wearable AI hardware like glasses or earbuds for OpenAI's major 2026 product announcement, driven by persistent supply chain reports of a screenless, audio-first device in collaboration with Foxconn and former Apple designers, with unveiling eyed for the second half amid massive $122 billion funding. Recent software releases, including GPT-5.5 last week—the company's strongest launch yet with surging API revenue—and April's ChatGPT Images 2.0 and agentic Codex expansions, underscore OpenAI's software dominance but heighten pressure for hardware differentiation against competitors like Google's Gemini wearables and Anthropic's agents. OpenAI DevDay on September 29 remains a key catalyst for resolution-defining reveals, though historical delays in consumer devices warrant caution on timelines.

markets.question: Will Houston Texans win the 2027 NFL AFC Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2027 AFC championship game.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2027 NFL AFC championship per the rules of the NFL (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2027 NFL AFC championship game is cancelled, postponed after March 31, 2027 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from NFL (https://www.nfl.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Buffalo's Josh Allen anchors the Bills at 12.5% with their favorable AFC East path and recent MVP contention, while Chiefs hold steady at 10.5% on Patrick Mahomes' pedigree despite potential roster turnover. Chargers (8.5%) benefit from Jim Harbaugh's scheme fitting Justin Herbert, keeping the top cluster tight as no offseason move created a clear separator in this quarterback-rich conference.

markets.question: Ethereal FDV above $600M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ethereal's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ethereal (https://x.com/etherealdex) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Jordan Spieth win the 2026 Cadillac Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Cadillac Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Cadillac Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Cadillac Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 9, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

Jordan L. Smith sits second at 18.3%, buoyed by consistent recent form, while Jordan Spieth and Alex Smalley lurk at T3 (-7) after 65s showcasing strong approach play. Round 2 developments see Nick Taylor grab clubhouse lead at -9 (thru 15), Si Woo Kim charging to -7 (thru 13), and J.T. Poston at -5, tightening the competitive field as weather holds favorable.
