markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 400-419 tweets from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 28 12:00 PM ET to May 5, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters around 140-199 posts for Elon Musk's X activity from April 28 to May 5, reflecting his recent high-volume pace of 22-30 daily posts, as seen on April 28 (22) and 29 (30), fueled by xAI's Grok 4.3 launch and replies on AI benchmarks, Tesla data cleaning, and political debates like noncitizen voting. This 8-day window projects 160-200 at current averages, but daily fluctuations—spiking on viral moments or dipping during focus shifts—keep 160-179 (20.3%), 140-159 (19.9%), and 180-199 (17.2%) tightly contested. Key swing factors include upcoming Tesla updates or election buzz through May 5, with no confirmed slowdown signals.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Melanie Stansbury (D) commands trader consensus at 91% implied probability to win New Mexico's 1st Congressional District, reflecting the seat's solid Democratic lean—demonstrated by strong partisan voting index and Harris's 2024 margin—and her fundraising dominance over Republican nominee Ndidiamaka "Didi" Okpareke, a pharmacist who secured the unopposed GOP nod in March. With no public polling indicating competitiveness and primaries set for June 2, the race aligns with historical incumbency advantages in safe districts. Challenges would require a Stansbury scandal, massive GOP national midterm wave, or Okpareke fundraising surge, though structural barriers remain high.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

South America's 20.5% reflects CONMEBOL's grueling standings where Argentina (defending champions) led ahead of Ecuador, Colombia, Uruguay, Brazil, and Paraguay. Asia (AFC's nine entrants including Japan, Australia, South Korea) and Africa (CAF's ten like Morocco, Senegal, Egypt) hold 2.9% each amid limited knockout success; North America's hosts plus Curaçao, Haiti, Panama at 2.1%, and Oceania's New Zealand at 0.3% face steep barriers. March 31 play-offs finalized the field without upsets boosting underdogs.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a razor-thin split, with 50-60B and 80-90B raise outcomes each commanding 50% implied probabilities, underscoring uncertainty in SpaceX's June 2026 IPO pricing amid its confidential S-1 filing on April 1. Recent tender offers pegged private valuations at $800 billion, building toward a targeted $1.75-2 trillion public debut, supported by Starlink's $15-16 billion annual revenue and $24.8 billion cash reserves funding Starship and AI capex. The deadlock hinges on investor demand for the dual-class structure preserving Elon Musk's control, final roadshow dynamics in early June, and macroeconomic risk appetite, with lower buckets pricing in potential market volatility or regulatory delays.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Cape Town be 25°C or higher on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Cape Town International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Cape Town International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/za/matroosfontein/FACT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 53% implied probability to an 18°C high in Cape Town on May 2, driven by the South African Weather Service (SAWS) forecast issued at 16:00 SAST on May 1, predicting fine and cool to warm conditions across the Western Cape with moderate to fresh southerly winds along the coast that moderate daytime peaks via sea-breeze enhancement from the cold Benguela Current. This aligns with short-range model ensembles showing limited solar heating under partial cloudiness and autumnal high-pressure dominance, following May 1's observed clustering around 18°C despite a 20°C forecast maximum. Typical May highs average 19°C historically, but current atmospheric stability caps upside risk; watch the SAWS 05:00 SAST update and real-time observations for potential refinements amid inherent forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Recent reports of a U.S. invitation for Putin to attend the December 2026 G20 summit in Miami fall well after resolution, reinforcing trader skepticism for near-term diplomacy amid ongoing Ukraine war negotiations and sanctions. Low odds on locations like China (4.8%) stem from faint speculation around neutral sites or Putin's regional travel, but lack concrete diplomatic signals or official commitments to shift the crowded wisdom-of-crowds pricing. Upcoming Ukraine peace talks could catalyze changes, though structural barriers like ICC warrants and allied pressures maintain the no-meeting lead.

markets.question: Spread: SC Freiburg (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SC Freiburg" if SC Freiburg win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfL Wolfsburg".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Freiburg vs. VfL Wolfsburg - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SC Freiburg holds a strong position in eighth place, riding a solid run of three recent wins and aiming to solidify mid-table security with home advantage at Europa-Park Stadion against relegation-threatened VfL Wolfsburg, who sit 17th amid a winless streak and just one victory in their last five matches. Wolfsburg's woes deepened yesterday with captain Maximilian Arnold ruled out due to a lingering groin injury, joining key absences like Jonas Wind (hamstring), Jenson Seelt (knee), and Kevin Paredes (muscle), thinning their squad for this survival scrap. Freiburg boasts balanced head-to-head history (14 wins to Wolfsburg's 13) and robust home form, while Wolfsburg's away struggles and injury crisis tilt trader consensus toward the hosts in this penultimate matchday clash.

markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (LOW) $147 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Game Handicap: NGX (-2.5) vs South America Rejects (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Grand final match between Nigma Galaxy and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 1 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy wins 3 or more games than South America Rejects in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "South America Rejects". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs South America Rejects (BO5) - DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Grand final match between Nigma Galaxy and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 1 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against South America Rejects.

This market will resolve to "South America Rejects" if South America Rejects win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy advanced undefeated through DreamLeague Division 2 Season 4 playoffs after a flawless 7-0 group stage (14-0 maps), sweeping Breeki Cheeki, Zero Tenacity, and 1w Team in upper bracket Bo3s, positioning them as trader favorites entering the BO5 grand final. South America Rejects, after a 1-2 upper bracket quarterfinal loss to 1w Team, staged a dominant lower bracket run with seven straight Bo3 victories—including sweeps over Zero Tenacity, Power Rangers, and 1w Team—building massive momentum and adaptability on the Europe West server. Both teams have secured spots in DreamLeague Season 29, but the winner claims first-place seeding and larger prize share from the $50,000 pool; Nigma's Davai missed early playoffs for personal reasons, with stand-ins stepping up effectively.

Thicknesse rebounded from a decision loss to Aleksandre Topuria with a unanimous decision win over Josias Musasa in September 2025, leveraging strong jiu-jitsu top control from elite camps like Volkanovski's crew, plus home-crowd energy and a three-inch leg-reach edge. markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart dated May 2, 2026, securing her spot among eight distinct artists topping the chart this year—including Taylor Swift (two songs), Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, Ella Langley (longest run with seven weeks on "Choosin' Texas"), Bad Bunny, BTS, and Mariah Carey. This streaming-fueled debut underscores pop's dominance amid country crossovers and K-pop surges, driven by viral TikTok traction and radio airplay. With summer releases looming from Drake ("Iceman"), A$AP Rocky ("Don't Be Dumb"), and Playboi Carti ("Baby Boi"), traders eye debut potential in a volatile landscape where weekly chart updates and promotional pushes could crown new frontrunners before year-end.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 23°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a maximum temperature of 21°C (38.5% implied probability) or 20°C (33.5%) in Madrid on May 3, reflecting the latest AEMET and ECMWF forecast ensembles showing an influx of cooler mid-level air masses from the northwest, following recent highs near 23°C on May 1. This shift introduces cloud cover and a 50%+ shower risk, capping daytime heating despite above-normal seasonal trends, with model spreads capturing uncertainty—drier outcomes could push toward 22°C, while persistent overcast skies favor 19-20°C. Climatological May 3 averages hover around 21-22°C, but near-term troughing dominates. Watch AEMET's daily updates and ECMWF refresh at 00Z for potential refinements ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Will Drake feature Lil Durk on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artists who feature on Drake's album "ICEMAN".

To qualify as "featured", the listed artist must be credited on at least one song on the album according to at least one major streaming platform: namely Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, or YouTube Music.

If the album fails to release by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source of this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 78% implied probability on "No" due to the absence of Department of Justice or FBI indictments explicitly charging an individual as an Iranian agent—such as an IRGC operative, spy, or covert operative—in espionage, assassination plots, or intelligence activities since the March 6 conviction of Asif Merchant in a prior murder-for-hire scheme. Recent cases, including the April 19 arrest of Shamim Mafi for sanctions violations in brokering Iranian arms sales to Sudan and the April 25 indictment of Jafar Tafakori for migrant smuggling, involve Iranian nationals but focus on economic crimes rather than qualifying agency allegations. With no fresh announcements in late April and the May 31 deadline nearing amid ongoing U.S. sanctions enforcement, markets anticipate no qualifying developments.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official observations confirm the highest air temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C at the Observatory station, locking in 100% market-implied probability for "25°C or higher" as traders align with this verified data from the authoritative source. Despite a cooler morning influenced by a lingering northeast monsoon and cloud band over Guangdong's coast—following a cold front on April 29 that dropped late-afternoon temperatures—the mainly cloudy conditions allowed sufficient daytime solar heating to surpass the 25°C threshold amid an exceptionally warm April overall, marked by southerly airstreams and anticyclonic influences earlier in the month. Revisions to finalized HKO daily extracts are not considered for resolution, rendering challenges like measurement errors negligible.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

Traders weigh persistent inflation risks against labor market cooling, with key catalysts including April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting potentially influencing easing expectations before 2027.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 500-519 tweets from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". events.title: Elon Musk # tweets April 24 - May 1, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the April 24 to May 1, 2026, period concluding at 12:00 PM ET today, trader consensus at 100% on 180-199 posts reflects real-time trackers like xTracker confirming Elon Musk's (@elonmusk) activity tallied 158 posts by April 30 morning across six days at a 26-post daily average, with May 1 contributions—covering xAI's Grok 4.3 launch, fertility rate concerns, and replies on policy topics—keeping the total firmly in range per public X data. This commanding position arises from his consistent posting pace of 20-30 daily amid high-visibility engagements, verifiable by any user. Only an implausible late surge exceeding 40 posts before cutoff could shift odds, as historical patterns show no such anomalies near resolution.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

markets.question: Will MrBeast say "Hundred" or "Thousand" or "Million" 5+ times during his next video?
markets.description:
MrBeast releases videos on https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mr Beast says the listed term during the next video he releases on YouTube. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old videos or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

If no such video is posted by MrBeast by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be the released video from (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast).

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

He clinched the party's nomination on April 14 after a hard-fought primary runoff against incumbent Jeonnam Governor Kim Yung-rok, buoyed by consistent polling leads in pre-runoff surveys showing him ahead by 10-13 points among party voters. People Power Party's Lee Jung-hyun, nominated April 22, trails at minimal odds despite pledging a "30% election revolution" to challenge the monopoly; historical precedents show conservative candidates garnering under 20% in similar races. Recent minor controversy over primary ARS outages was dismissed by the party. Upsets would require a major scandal, health issue for Min, or national wave shifting turnout dramatically in the final month.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent frustrations with NATO allies, voiced amid the ongoing Iran conflict and demands for higher defense spending, have fueled speculation about a potential US withdrawal, with White House spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt confirming on April 9 that the president is actively discussing the option ahead of talks with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte. However, Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act prohibits unilateral executive withdrawal, mandating a two-thirds Senate vote or congressional approval—a high bar given bipartisan support for the alliance. Europe has accelerated contingency plans, including potential war drills, while NATO weighs scaling back annual summits to avoid confrontation. No formal notice under Article 13 has been issued, and congressional leaders like Senate Minority Leader Schumer affirm opposition, underscoring significant institutional barriers before any deadline.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Anthropic an 80.5% implied probability of holding the #1 spot on the Style Control On leaderboard— a Chatbot Arena mode that prioritizes substantive content over stylistic flair—by May 31, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's April 16 launch, which propelled it to the top with superior reasoning and coding benchmarks like SWE-Bench Pro. markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades around 1.176 amid renewed USD strength following the Federal Reserve's April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, where policymakers held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid resilient U.S. inflation and labor data, widening policy divergence with the European Central Bank. ECB's more accommodative stance, with deposit rates at 2.00%, has pressured the pair below recent 1.1800 resistance, reflecting trader consensus on sustained U.S. yield advantages via 10-year Treasury levels near 4.2%. Eurozone GDP growth lags U.S. expansion, but forecasts eye potential EUR/USD upside to 1.20+ by year-end if Fed delivers one-two 25 basis point cuts. Watch June 16-17 FOMC and July ECB meetings for rate path signals, alongside May nonfarm payrolls and eurozone CPI releases.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, marking the third consecutive pause amid elevated core PCE inflation at 3.2% in March and solid economic growth tempered by softening job gains. March dot plot projections indicate a median 3.4% rate by year-end 2026 and low-3% levels by 2027, aligning with CME FedWatch Tool's market-implied path near 3.6% through mid-year. Traders weigh persistent inflation risks against labor market cooling, with key catalysts including April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting potentially influencing easing expectations before 2027.

Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93% implied probability against direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, driven by Pyongyang's entrenched hostility and prioritization of military advancements over dialogue. North Korea's multiple short-range ballistic missile tests on April 19 underscored ongoing escalation, while Kim Jong Un's March formalization of South Korea as its "primary hostile state" remains the dominant policy framework, blocking bilateral engagement. Recent high-level visits from Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin on April 27 and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on April 10 highlight Pyongyang's deepening alliances with Moscow and Beijing, diverting focus from Seoul. South Korea's early April apology for a drone incursion elicited a mild North Korean response but no reciprocal overtures or scheduled negotiations, reinforcing structural barriers absent late-breaking diplomatic shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Elon Bull Run Parlay, with "No" at an 88% implied probability, reflects the compounded unlikelihood of all three 2026 milestones aligning: Elon Musk reaching trillionaire status per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches to 100 km altitude. As of early May, Musk's net worth hovers around $800 billion—buoyed by Tesla's $573 million in sales to xAI and SpaceX but far from doubling amid volatile markets—while no new baby has been announced. Starship progress lags with just one qualifying flight (Flight 12 on April 7), despite Flight 13 slated for early May and FAA approvals for up to 44 launches annually; historical cadence and technical hurdles temper expectations for the required pace. Recent Tesla-xAI investments signal synergies, but traders see slim odds for the full parlay by year-end.

Breman's dismal form—losing over half their games—and defensive frailties are exacerbated by injuries to forward Victor Boniface (knee, fitness concerns), defender Mitchell Weiser (long-term), Felix Agu (muscle), and Karim Coulibaly (hamstring), plus potential suspensions like Cameron Puertas. markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 60-79 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Sen. Lindsey Graham commands 86% trader consensus as the South Carolina Republican Senate primary winner on June 9, driven by his overwhelming fundraising advantage—over $20 million raised—paired with President Trump's reaffirmed endorsement on April 10, which bolstered his incumbency edge in a state favoring established conservatives. Challenger Mark Lynch holds 11% implied probability after Paul Dans, Project 2025 architect, withdrew from the race that day and endorsed him, consolidating anti-Graham support amid a crowded field of 12 Republicans; however, polls like a March Pulse survey (Graham 41%, Lynch 21%) underscore Graham's lead despite no majority yet secured. Low shares for Paul Dans (0.8%) and Thomas Murphy (0.1%) reflect their minimal viability post-consolidation. Early voting and turnout in key areas like Greenville could influence a potential runoff.

markets.question: Portsmouth FC vs. Birmingham City FC: O/U 3.5

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Jerome Powell depart as Fed Chair before May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date range during which Jerome Powell vacates his role as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of Powell's resignation or firing will not alone qualify.

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's April 29 confirmation that he will step down from the chair position on May 15, at the end of his four-year term, while remaining on the Board of Governors through 2028, has solidified trader consensus at 95% for departure in the May 15–22 window. This follows President Trump's public pressure for an earlier exit and signals of a successor nomination, such as Kevin Warsh, amid policy differences on inflation and rates. The market reflects the legal protections of Fed independence, with Powell rejecting resignation requests. Potential shifts could arise from a surprise early resignation, successful firing attempt despite legal hurdles, or abrupt re-nomination and Senate confirmation, though historical precedents favor term-end transitions.

markets.question: Will MetaMask launch a token by September 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Metamask officially launches a token by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Metamask, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.
